events.raw_text: Wed, Jun 10 · 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM PDT. Lightning talks: Vince Faller on dbt-wizard; Hamzah Chaudhary demoing Lightdash Data Apps; Luca Martial demoing ktx by Kaelio; Karen Hsieh on an agentic workflow for data requests.

talks.description: Vince Faller presents dbt-wizard, an opinionated pattern for metadata checks in dbt, and invites feedback on the approach.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Vince Faller on dbt-wizard — an opinionated pattern for metadata checks in dbt. He's curious what people think about the approach.
talks.title: dbt-wizard: an opinionated pattern for metadata checks in dbt

talks.description: Hamzah Chaudhary demos Lightdash Data Apps, which turn a prompt into a governed data app backed by your semantic layer.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Hamzah Chaudhary demoing Lightdash Data Apps: type a prompt, get a governed data app backed by your semantic layer. Beta just opened in May.
talks.title: Lightdash Data Apps demo

talks.description: Luca Martial demos ktx by Kaelio, an open-source context layer that lets AI agents query data accurately, built on dbt.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Luca Martial demoing ktx by Kaelio (YC X25): open-source context layer so AI agents can query your data accurately —built on top of dbt.
talks.title: ktx by Kaelio demo

talks.description: Karen Hsieh talks through an agentic workflow she uses to keep up with data requests.

talks.title: An agentic workflow for keeping up with data requests

events.agenda: General Discussion and Q&A on Transformation Strategy; 3 Breakout Sessions running concurrently; Review of breakout sessions; Close and Q&A.
events.event_description: An open discussion of where transformation could and should occur (ETL vs ELT vs tETLTt), what use case makes the most sense with each type of transformation, and interesting edge cases where data is transformed in unusual ways.
events.event_name: San Francisco dbt Meetup - ONLINE September Edition
events.raw_text: Fri, Sep 17, 2021 · 12:00 AM to 1:30 AM CEST. Transformation Strategy: tETLTt - open discussion on where transformation could and should occur, followed by 3 breakout sessions. Speakers/organizers: Callum McCann, Anmol Singh, Joel McKelvey.

talks.description: An open discussion on where transformation could and should occur across the data stack, followed by breakout sessions on selected topics.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: This session will be an open discussion of where transformation could and should occur, what use case makes the most sense with each type of transformation, and some interesting edge cases where data is transformed in unusual ways.
talks.speaker_name: Callum McCann, Anmol Singh, Joel McKelvey
talks.speaker_title: Solutions Architect; Application Engineer; VP Product Marketing
talks.title: Transformation Strategy: tETLTt

events.agenda: Panel discussion with data industry leaders on improving data team practices and the evolution of the modern data stack.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are gatherings dedicated to helping you own your analytics engineering workflow. Events focus on the impact of dbt in the data community and professional development tools for analytics engineers.
events.raw_text: Thu, May 6, 2021 · 9:00 PM to 10:15 PM CEST. Panel: Julia Schottenstein (Fishtown Analytics), Sameer Al-Sakran (Metabase), Boris Jabes (Census), Gleb Mezhanskiy (Datafold). Moderated by Mila Page (Fishtown Analytics).

talks.description: A panel of data industry leaders offering advice on improving data team practices and discussing the evolution of the modern data stack.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: We'll be joined by: Julia Schottenstein, Product Manager, Fishtown Analytics; Sameer Al-Sakran, CEO, Metabase; Boris Jabes, CEO, Census; Gleb Mezhanskiy, CEO & Co-Founder, Datafold. The one and only, Mila Page will moderate the discussion.
talks.speaker_name: Julia Schottenstein, Sameer Al-Sakran, Boris Jabes, Gleb Mezhanskiy
talks.title: Data Industry Leaders Panel

additional_metadata.note: First online-only San Francisco meetup, held during COVID-19.

events.raw_text: Thu, Jul 30, 2020 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CEST. Responding in an emergency with data, DataSF (Tania Jogesh, Blake Valenta, Jason Lally). Improving data reliability using tests in dbt, Evgeny Rubtsov (Zoox).

talks.description: How DataSF responded to the COVID-19 emergency and provided a 360-degree view of PPE inventory, using dbt, agile processes, and empathy.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Responding in an emergency with data 🎤
Tania Jogesh, Data Scientist, DataSF ... Discover how DataSF responded to the Covid-19 emergency and provided a 360-degree view of PPE (personal protective equipment) inventory.
talks.speaker_name: Tania Jogesh, Blake Valenta, Jason Lally
talks.speaker_title: DataSF, City & County of San Francisco
talks.title: Responding in an emergency with data

talks.description: How Zoox ensures data integrity with dbt: testing at runtime, schema tests, and snapshots to catch source data changes.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Improving data reliability using tests in dbt 🎤
Evgeny Rubtsov, Data Engineer, Zoox ... Learn how Zoox ensures data integrity with dbt: testing at runtime (vs. CI/CD), using schema tests
talks.title: Improving data reliability using tests in dbt

additional_metadata.venue_host: Envoy, 410 Townsend St 4th floor, San Francisco

events.agenda: Doors open at 6pm for a 6:30 start. Four talks: Building trust in your data; How to survive as a data team of one; Modeling pricing configuration data; Glue work in analytics.
events.event_description: This event is hosted by Envoy and open to all folks working with data—predominantly focused on people's experience with dbt, but also broader topics related to analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.location: Envoy, 410 Townsend St 4th floor, San Francisco, CA
events.raw_text: Tue, Nov 5, 2019 · 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM PST. Building trust in your data (Andrea Koptiz, Envoy). How to survive as a data team of one (Martin Guindon, Absolunet.com). Modeling pricing configuration data (Tyler Pugliese, Fastly). Glue work in analytics (Caitlin Moorman, Yerdle Recommerce).

talks.description: How Envoy used human feedback and a rigorous testing process to debug financial models and build trust in financial data.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Building trust in your data
Andrea Koptiz, Data Analyst, Envoy ... Learn how Envoy used human feedback along with a rigorous testing process to debug financial models.
talks.title: Building trust in your data

talks.description: Lessons learned surviving as a one-person analytics team, from tooling choices to working with non-data stakeholders.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
How to survive as a data team of one
Martin Guindon, Asst. Director, BI, Absolunet.com ... This talk will cover a range of lessons learned–from choosing the right tooling to working effectively with non-data team members.
talks.title: How to survive as a data team of one

talks.description: How Fastly used dbt to turn raw Salesforce CPQ pricing configuration data into a polished dataset for sales pipeline analytics.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Modeling pricing configuration data
Tyler Pugliese, Fastly ... Learn how the data team used dbt to turn raw, isolated CPQ data into a polished dataset
talks.title: Modeling pricing configuration data

talks.description: A discussion of "glue work" in analytics: valuable but under-recognized work, and how to get it valued and rewarded.
talks.raw_text_excerpt:
Glue work in analytics
Caitlin Moorman, Yerdle Recommerce ... When work is valuable but not valued, it becomes glue work. How do you get un-stuck and make sure your work is recognized and rewarded?
talks.title: Glue work in analytics
